concept: Waste / Material or Energy Assigned No Further Intended Use
description: Material or energy assigned no further intended use. The status is relational, not inherent: the same fallen branch can be waste to one process and, in the same afternoon, habitat material to another.
sound_symbolism: The complete hollow opening 'whemo', shared with 'whemoa' (empty), recalls apparent absence of intended use. Final 'ri' turns the remainder back into relation with a holder, process, place, or future use, so the word does not end in annihilation.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun: 'ha muralo whemori ma nai.' (This material is not waste.) State whose process assigns the status, present condition, intended next action, and affected place or beings. Distinct from 'whemoa' (empty), 'holume' (loss), 'hisophi' (contaminant), and 'panuri' (resource).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Waste is made discussable without treating disposal as disappearance or assuming that another use automatically makes a material safe or sustainable.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The definition resists an inherent waste essence and makes the designation dependent on changing relations, intentions, and conditions.

tags.community: a status assigned by a holder, process, or institution with consequences for others
tags.physical: material or energy lacking further intended use in its present relation
